title: Lune Oddo Divinian

'''Lune Oddo Divinian''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was the son of [[Astri Oddo]] and [[Bog Divinian]]. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Divinian received basic [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] as well as the [[Jedi Code/Legends|tenets]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] by fugitive [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Ry-Gaul]] and [[Garen Muln]]. After both mentors were killed, Divinian lived on as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] until eventually joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
[[File:Oddo_and_Flume.jpg|thumb|left|Lune Oddo Divinian and Trever Flume in a starfighter simulator]]
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Lune Oddo Divinian was the son of [[Bog Divinian]] and [[Astri Oddo]] and was born on the planet [[Nuralee]] in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. For reasons unknown, Divinian's sensitivity to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] was not detected at birth, therefore missing an opportunity to study as a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. During Divinian's formative years, his father was elected [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] of his homeworld and the family began to spend time on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] where Divinian stayed in the care of his grandfather, [[Didi Oddo]]. Around this time Divinian's mother began to note her son's affinity for using the Force, keeping this fact a secret from her husband who loathed the Jedi Order and Force-users. Despite her efforts, Senator Divinian witnessed his son's talents and he began to plot ways to exploit his son.<ref name="TFP" />

As Divinian's father began to become more hostile his mother gathered a few belongings and the mother and child fled to the planet [[Samaria/Legends|Samaria]] to live in relative peace. During the years that followed the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] and the Republic, but the two managed to avoid the atrocities of war. When the Republic and the Confederacy came out on the loosing end of the war and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] rose to galactic dominance Divinian's father, Bog Divinian was assigned to the Samarian government as an [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisor]]. Realizing the danger they were in, Astri Oddo approached the Samarian [[Prime Minister/Legends|Prime Minister]] [[Aaren Larker]] from who she learned of her estranged husband's plot to capture their son and turn him over to the Empire for education at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]]. With stolen codes to a [[Platform-7]] supercomputer, Astri Oddo was able to sabotage the equipment and escape the planet with her son.<ref name="ATW">''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]''</ref>

Not only the Empire was interested in Divinian and his mother after the disabling of the platform; the family was approached by former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Ferus Olin]] in the following weeks and was impressed by the boy's abilities that he exhibited.<ref name="ATW" /> Olin agreed to take the pair to his secret [[Jedi asteroid|asteroid base]] far from the eyes of the Empire, where former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Garen Muln]] assisted Divinian in learning to use and control [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="ROTDS" />

===Coruscant===
During their stay on the asteroid, the Divinian's grew comfortable with their fellow refugees living in relative safety and peace. However, when [[Trever Flume]] returned to the base with activist [[Eve Yarrow|Flame]], Astri Oddo began to think that their sanctuary could wind up targeted by the Empire sooner then expected. Fearing for her son's safety, the two departed the asteroid and went to Coruscant under the guardianship of Jedi Master [[Fy-Tor-Ana]] and [[Clive Flax]]. Seeking out the [[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]], an operative working against the Empire, Divinian's mother hoped that the [[Besalisk/Legends|Besalisk]] could get them off planet and too a safe world. While his mother met with Jettster, Divinian went to a park with [[Keets Freely]] and [[Curran Caladian]] where he modified the [[power slide]] to shoot him up higher and did somersaults with the Force. Detecting the boy's use of the Force, a squad of passing [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] attempted to seize Divinian but were unable to get to him before his guardians were able to make an escape. The escape was short-lived however; the stormtroopers managed to capture the boy and he was escorted to the [[Imperial Naval Academy (Coruscant)|Imperial Naval Academy]] nearby where his father was awaiting him.<ref name="The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]''</ref>

===The Imperial Academy===
At the Academy, Divinian began being indoctrinated in Imperial Naval life. After spending some time at the academy, [[Trever Flume]] was enlisted to rescue him. In one class, they were able to best one of the older cadets, [[Kestrel]]; however, Divinian's father received word from [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Sano Sauro]] that [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] needed someone for a "special project." When Bog Divinian decided to send his son, both Divinian and Flume tried to escape but were caught by [[Maggis]], the head instructor. The two were separated and Divinian met with his father again who turned him over to Vader for the project. As Divinian was escorted off the premises with Vader, the boy was made aware that he was to be a test subject for a memory-altering drug created by the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. Before the drug could be applied, Divinian was rescued by Ferus Olin and, after jumping from a window, they were both rescued by Flume and a defective Maggis. Safely away from his father and Vader, Divinian then returned to the asteroid to continue his training with Master [[Garen Muln]]. Peace was short-lived once more, as Vader led an assault group against the asteroid. Before the base and all its occupants were destroyed, the three Jedi stationed there loaded Divinian into an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]], ensuring that he would live to grow and carry on their cause.<ref name="The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]''</ref>

===Mercenary===
[[File:LuneDivinian2.jpg|thumb|left|Lune Divinian in 0 ABY.]]

Having been reunited with his mother after escaping the asteroid, Divinian was raised by Astri Oddo and [[Clive Flax]], along with a purposefully brainwashed Trever Flume on a world far from the Empire's reach. However, his parents continued to involve themselves in anti-Imperial works. They and Trever apparently died on one mission.

By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Divinian was operating as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] under the pseudonym 'Div'. He was one of many mercenaries hired by [[X-7]] to hunt down [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. After crash landing on the planet and assisting Skywalker, he was taken prisoner and held on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], who wanted to know where to find his employer. Imperial agents later abducted him from his cell, and Skywalker left to find him.<ref name="Rebel Force: Trapped">''[[Rebel Force: Trapped]]''</ref>

Upon being found by Skywalker, the pair fought off attacking Imperials and, with the assistance of Ferus Olin, escaped to a small asteroid. Once safely there, Olin knocked Skywalker unconscious and caught up with Divinian, filling each other in on their past few years they had spent separated. Assisting Skywalker back to a Alliance base, Olin and Divinian both joined the Alliance formally and agreed to hunt down the assassin X-7. On Yavin IV Divinian gave at least one lesson in [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] combat to Luke.

During the job, Olin convinced Divinian to trick X-7 into thinking he was Trever Flume, the pair's old friend and Divinian's adopted brother who had apparently died years earlier. In the process of doing so, Divinian became convinced of this himself. While their ruse worked, the confused X-7 turned on Olin and Divinian, shooting Divinian in the shoulder and engaging Olin. When Olin stabbed X-7 in the heart, the assassin begged Divinian to say that he mattered. Divinian consents, recognizing that whether or not X-7 had been Trever, he once had a family that he mattered to.

===Death===
On his final mission, "Div" sacrificed himself in battle. He crashed his crippled starfighter into a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] in an attempt to give Alliance members [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], Luke Skywalker and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] time to escape the Imperial fleet and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. His mission complete, Divinian's crippled, burning starfighter plummeted down the gravity well of a nearby world. Divinian either perished in the ensuing crash, or in the system-consuming explosion soon after.<ref name="Rebel Force: Uprising"> ''[[Rebel Force: Uprising]]''</ref>

== Behind the scenes ==
Lune Oddo Divinian first appeared in the [[2004]] young-readers novel ''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]''.<ref name="TFP" /> Lune's age has varied in the [[Star Wars: The Last of the Jedi|''Last of the Jedi'']] books. In ''[[The Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web|Last of the Jedi: A Tangled Web]]'', he is described as looking about eight years old.<ref name="ATW" /> In ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Return of the Dark Side|Last of the Jedi: Return of the Dark Side]]'' he is referred to as being six years old.<ref name="ROTDS" /> In ''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon|Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' he is described as being eight years old, despite the book only taking place a few weeks after Return of the Dark Side.<ref name="The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon" /> All the books following refer to Lune as being definitively eight years old.<ref name="The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire" /><ref name="Master of Deception">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]''</ref><ref name="Reckoning">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]''</ref>
